name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property and retail merchandise sales.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc., headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, is the dominant player in the pawn industry globally. Founded and operating as a publicly traded company (component of S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 indices), FirstCash has built a massive retail footprint serving cash and credit-constrained consumers primarily through pawn transactions. The company employs approximately 22,000 people across its U.S., Latin American, and UK operations, making it a major employer in the consumer lending space.

FirstCash's core business revolves around non-recourse pawn loans secured by personal property pledges. Customers can obtain fast cash by pawning jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and other merchandise. Beyond pawn loans, the company operates retail sales of the same merchandise categories, layaway services with 10% down payment options, and gold/precious metal buying services. Through its wholly-owned subsidiary AFF, FirstCash also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions across a network of 15,000+ retail merchant partners, diversifying revenue beyond traditional pawn operations.

What distinguishes FirstCash is its scale and international reach—3,300+ locations across 29 states plus DC, the UK, Mexico (all states), Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador represent unmatched geographic coverage in the pawn industry. The company's integration into major stock indices reflects institutional credibility unusual for the pawn sector. Their ancillary services (layaway, gold buying, retail financing) create multiple revenue streams and customer touchpoints beyond basic pawn lending. The subsidiary AFF's point-of-sale payment solutions indicate technological sophistication beyond typical pawn shops.

However, pawn loans remain fundamentally high-cost borrowing despite being non-recourse. Interest rates and fees on pawn loans are not disclosed on the website, making cost comparison impossible upfront. While pawn avoids credit checks and serves genuinely credit-constrained populations, borrowers lose possession of items immediately and must repay within defined periods or forfeit collateral. The massive scale, while impressive operationally, doesn't change the economic reality that pawn is a last-resort funding mechanism for cash-strapped consumers.
pros: Fast cash access without credit checks or income verification required
Non-recourse loans—borrowers only lose pledged collateral, not subject to deficiency judgments
Massive 3,300+ store network across 29 U.S. states, DC, UK, and Latin America for convenience
Wide variety of accepted collateral items (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances, sporting goods)
Gold and precious metals buying service provides immediate cash for items many consumers have on hand
Layaway service option with affordable 10% down payment for budget-conscious shoppers
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) indicating regulatory oversight and transparency
No impact on credit score or credit report—non-credit-based lending
cons: Interest rates and fees not disclosed on website, preventing transparent cost comparison before borrowing
Immediate loss of possession of pledged items creates inconvenience and lifestyle disruption
Pawn loans are fundamentally high-cost borrowing designed only for short-term cash emergencies
Limited repayment flexibility—standard pawn terms don't accommodate extended payment periods like personal loans
Retail merchandise quality and selection varies significantly by location, as noted by 'you never know what you'll find' marketing language
best_for: Cash-strapped consumers needing immediate funds without credit checks or debt obligation
People with valuable items (jewelry, electronics, instruments) willing to temporarily forfeit them for quick loans
Shoppers seeking affordable layaway options with minimal down payment (10%) to spread purchases
Individuals with damaged credit who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit products
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for individuals facing immediate cash emergencies who have valuable personal property to pledge and cannot qualify for or access traditional credit products. The critical caveat is that pawn loans are inherently expensive short-term borrowing mechanisms—while non-recourse structure limits downside risk, the cost of obtaining cash this way is significantly higher than alternatives like personal loans, payday alternative programs, or credit union advances, making this appropriate only when other options are unavailable.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments, sporting goods)
Retail sales of new and used merchandise across all categories
Gold, silver, and platinum buying—immediate cash payment for precious metals
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Store locator with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states and DC
Online inventory browsing and item hunting across store network
Lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ merchant partners)
Point-of-sale payment technology solutions for retail partners
Pawn FAQs and educational resources on pawn process
Fast pawn loan approval and cash disbursement
